claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know that Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand a bit more about your situation.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning you provide for his needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for his care - I provide everything he needs like food, clothes, and a home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Has anyone else already claimed Child Benefit for Alex?** (Only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Do you have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are there any other circumstances that might affect your right to reside in the UK?** (For example, if you've recently moved to the UK, or if you're a non-UK/non-Irish citizen with a specific immigration status)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. I'm just a regular UK resident, no special immigration status or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for his care and provide for his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You are a regular UK resident

You meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex. 

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you'd like to proceed with your claim, you can apply for Child Benefit through the appropriate government channels.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. He lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for his care by providing food, clothes, and accommodation. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is important since only one person can claim for the same child. You are a regular UK resident with no immigration status issues that would affect your right to reside. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, they are responsible for Alex who is under 16 years old and lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. There are no circumstances that would prevent them from claiming.

He lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for his care by providing food, clothes, and accommodation. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is important since only one person can claim for the same child. You are a regular UK resident with no immigration status issues that would affect your right to reside. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

There are no circumstances that would prevent them from claiming.
